blocks.body: Typical passive voice constructions are special cases and idiomatic expressions with the passive. Here you will learn constructions with get, have something done, the passive with two objects, impersonal passive structures, and other useful features.

{"level":"B2","intro":"In this topic you will learn special passive voice constructions: get passive, have something done, the passive with two objects, and impersonal passive structures.","rules":[{"label":"Get Passive","color":"emerald","text":"A conversational variant: get + V3:","example":"He got fired last week."},{"label":"Causative","color":"blue","text":"Causative structure: have/get + object + V3:","example":"I had my car repaired."},{"label":"Impersonal","color":"rose","text":"Impersonal structures: It is said that...:","example":"It is believed that he is innocent."}]}

{"title":"1. Get Passive — conversational passive","sections":[{"label":"Structure","color":"emerald","description":"Formula: get + Past Participle (V3). It is more conversational than be + V3.","examples":[{"en":"He got fired last week.","ua":"He got fired last week."},{"en":"She got promoted to manager.","ua":"She got promoted to manager."},{"en":"They got married in June.","ua":"They got married in June."}]},{"label":"Use","color":"sky","description":"The get passive is often used for unexpected or negative events.","examples":[{"en":"The window got broken during the storm.","ua":"The window got broken during the storm."},{"en":"I got stuck in traffic.","ua":"I got stuck in traffic."},{"en":"She got hurt in the accident.","ua":"She got hurt in the accident."}]},{"label":"Tense forms","color":"amber","description":"Get changes with tense: get/gets/got/will get + V3.","examples":[{"en":"He often gets invited to parties.","ua":"He often gets invited to parties."},{"en":"She will get paid tomorrow.","ua":"She will get paid tomorrow."}]}]}

{"title":"2. Have/Get Something Done — causative construction","sections":[{"label":"Have + object + V3","color":"emerald","description":"When someone does something for us (a service, paid work, etc.).","examples":[{"en":"I had my car repaired.","ua":"I had my car repaired."},{"en":"She had her hair cut.","ua":"She had her hair cut."},{"en":"We had the house painted.","ua":"We had the house painted."}]},{"label":"Get + object + V3","color":"sky","description":"A more conversational version of have something done.","examples":[{"en":"I need to get my phone fixed.","ua":"I need to get my phone fixed."},{"en":"She got her nails done.","ua":"She got her nails done."},{"en":"We should get the roof checked.","ua":"We should get the roof checked."}]},{"label":"Negative experience","color":"rose","description":"It is also used for unpleasant situations.","examples":[{"en":"He had his wallet stolen.","ua":"He had his wallet stolen."},{"en":"She had her car broken into.","ua":"She had her car broken into."},{"en":"They had their house flooded.","ua":"They had their house flooded."}]}]}

{"title":"3. The passive with two objects","sections":[{"label":"Two variants","color":"emerald","description":"Verbs such as give, send, offer, tell, show take two objects. Both of them can become the subject in the passive.","examples":[{"en":"Active: They gave me a book.","ua":"They gave me a book."},{"en":"Passive 1: I was given a book.","ua":"I was given a book. (focus on me)"},{"en":"Passive 2: A book was given to me.","ua":"A book was given to me. (focus on the book)"}]},{"label":"The first variant is more common","color":"sky","description":"In English, a personal subject in the passive is usually more natural.","examples":[{"en":"She was offered a job. (better)","ua":"She was offered a job."},{"en":"A job was offered to her. (less common)","ua":"A job was offered to her."},{"en":"He was told the news.","ua":"He was told the news."}]}]}

{"title":"4. Impersonal passive constructions","sections":[{"label":"It + passive + that","color":"emerald","description":"Formal style: It is said/believed/known/reported that...","examples":[{"en":"It is said that he is very rich.","ua":"It is said that he is very rich."},{"en":"It is believed that the Earth is flat. (a mistaken belief)","ua":"It is believed that the Earth is flat. (a mistaken belief)"},{"en":"It is known that smoking causes cancer.","ua":"It is known that smoking causes cancer."}]},{"label":"Subject + passive + to","color":"sky","description":"Alternative structure: Subject + is said/believed + to + verb","examples":[{"en":"He is said to be very rich.","ua":"He is said to be very rich."},{"en":"She is believed to have left the country.","ua":"She is believed to have left the country."},{"en":"They are reported to be negotiating.","ua":"They are reported to be negotiating."}]},{"label":"Typical verbs","color":"amber","description":"Commonly used verbs: say, believe, think, know, report, expect, suppose, consider.","examples":[{"en":"The meeting is expected to start at 9.","ua":"The meeting is expected to start at 9."},{"en":"He is thought to be the best candidate.","ua":"He is thought to be the best candidate."}]}]}

{"title":"5. Born — a special construction","sections":[{"label":"Be born","color":"emerald","description":"Be born is always passive. The past form is was/were born.","examples":[{"en":"I was born in Kyiv.","ua":"I was born in Kyiv."},{"en":"She was born in 1990.","ua":"She was born in 1990."},{"en":"Where were you born?","ua":"Where were you born?"}]},{"label":"Present tense","color":"sky","description":"The present form am/is/are born is used for general facts.","examples":[{"en":"Babies are born every day.","ua":"Babies are born every day."},{"en":"About 140 million babies are born each year.","ua":"About 140 million babies are born each year."}]}]}

{"title":"6. Be Passive vs Get Passive","intro":"A comparison of the standard passive and the get passive:","rows":[{"en":"Be + V3","ua":"Neutral, formal","note":"The report was completed."},{"en":"Get + V3","ua":"Conversational, informal","note":"The report got completed."},{"en":"Be + V3","ua":"State or result","note":"The door is closed. (state)"},{"en":"Get + V3","ua":"Process, change","note":"The door got closed. (action)"}],"warning":"📌 The get passive is more common with unexpected, negative, or dynamic events!"}

{"title":"7. Quick summary","items":["Get passive (get + V3) is a conversational form, often used for unexpected events.","Have/Get something done is used when someone does something for us.","The passive with two objects allows either object to become the subject.","It is said/believed that... are formal impersonal structures.","Subject + is said + to... is an alternative impersonal passive structure.","Be born is always passive: I was born in...","Typical verbs: say, believe, think, know, report, expect, suppose."]}
